description: Comprehensive search engine, answer engine, and generative engine optimization for claru.ai. Fixes critical indexing gaps (SSR soft 404, blocked AI crawlers), adds Organization schema, llms.txt, canonical URLs, dynamic OG images, FAQ structured data, and question-form H2s. Goal: get all 82 pages indexed and make Claru citable by ChatGPT, Perplexity, Claude, and Google AI Overviews.

userStories.title: Create robots.ts — unblock AI crawlers, add Sitemap directive
userStories.description: As a site owner, I want robots.txt to allow AI search/citation crawlers while blocking training crawlers, so Claru pages appear in ChatGPT, Perplexity, Claude, and Google AI Overviews.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/app/robots.ts using Next.js MetadataRoute.Robots convention
Include Sitemap: https://claru.ai/sitemap.xml directive
Allow all standard crawlers (Googlebot, Bingbot) — default Allow: /
Explicitly allow AI search/citation crawlers: GPTBot, ClaudeBot, Claude-SearchBot, Claude-User, PerplexityBot, Applebot-Extended
Block AI training crawlers: Google-Extended, CCBot, Bytespider, meta-externalagent
Disallow /admin/, /portal/, /experiment/, /api/ for all user agents
Build succeeds; verify /robots.txt output matches expectations
Unit Tests: verify robots.ts export returns correct structure with all user-agent rules
Code Review: run code-reviewer agent to verify crawler policy correctness
userStories.notes: Read tasks/docs/seo-aeo-geo-requirements.md (Requirement 1) and tasks/docs/seo-aeo-geo-design.md (robots.txt section). No robots.txt or robots.ts currently exists; only metadata.robots in layout.tsx. Use general-purpose agent.

userStories.title: Add Organization + WebSite JSON-LD to root layout
userStories.description: As a site owner, I need root-level entity schemas so Google Knowledge Graph and LLMs recognize Claru as a named entity.
acceptanceCriteria: Add <script type='application/ld+json'> to src/app/layout.tsx body with @graph array
Organization schema includes: name 'Claru', legalName 'Reka AI Inc.', url 'https://claru.ai', logo (absolute URL), description, contactPoint with email team@claru.ai
Organization includes sameAs array: LinkedIn company page, GitHub org (https://github.com/claruai)
Organization includes knowsAbout array with relevant AI/ML topics
WebSite schema includes: name 'Claru', url 'https://claru.ai'
Schema validates at schema.org validator
Typecheck passes
Unit Tests: verify JSON-LD structure renders valid JSON with all required fields
Code Review: run code-reviewer agent to verify schema correctness
userStories.notes: Read tasks/docs/seo-aeo-geo-requirements.md (Requirement 2) and tasks/docs/seo-aeo-geo-design.md (Organization + WebSite Schema Architecture). Use the entity profile from US-012 as single source of truth if implemented first. Use general-purpose agent.

userStories.title: Create llms.txt for AI engine discoverability
userStories.description: As a site owner, I need an llms.txt file so AI systems can efficiently map and cite Claru's content.
acceptanceCriteria: Create public/llms.txt as a static Markdown file
Include site name, canonical company description, and primary URL
Map all major sections with URLs and 1-2 sentence descriptions: homepage, case studies, jobs, data catalog, pillar pages, sub-pillar pages, for-annotators, legal pages
Follow llms.txt spec format (title, description, ## Sections with URLs)
File accessible at /llms.txt after build
Code Review: run code-reviewer agent to verify format and completeness
userStories.notes: Read tasks/docs/seo-aeo-geo-requirements.md (Requirement 3) and tasks/docs/seo-aeo-geo-design.md (llms.txt Structure). See https://llmstxt.org for spec. Use general-purpose agent.

userStories.title: Add canonical URLs to pages missing them
userStories.description: As a site owner, I need canonical URLs on all public pages to prevent duplicate content signals.
acceptanceCriteria: Add alternates: { canonical: '/path' } to metadata in: data-catalog/layout.tsx, case-studies/layout.tsx, for-annotators/layout.tsx, data/layout.tsx, labeling/layout.tsx, privacy/page.tsx, terms/page.tsx, prohibited-use/page.tsx, job-applicant-privacy/page.tsx
Use relative paths — metadataBase in root layout resolves to full URLs
Do NOT add canonical to portal/admin/experiment pages
Build succeeds; verify <link rel='canonical'> renders in page source
Typecheck passes
Code Review: run code-reviewer agent to verify all canonicals are correct
userStories.notes: Read tasks/docs/seo-aeo-geo-plan.md (Phase 1, Task 4). Follow existing pattern from pillar layouts e.g. src/app/pillars/acquire/layout.tsx. Some pages use layout.tsx for metadata, others use page.tsx — check each. Use general-purpose agent.

userStories.title: Add dateModified to all structured data schemas
userStories.description: As a site owner, I need freshness signals in structured data so AI engines and Google prioritize our content.
acceptanceCriteria: Add dateModified to Article JSON-LD in src/app/case-studies/[slug]/page.tsx — use cs.dateModified ?? cs.datePublished
Add dateModified field to CaseStudy type if not already present
Add dateModified to Service JSON-LD in all 16 pillar/sub-pillar layout files
Create shared BUILD_DATE constant in src/lib/constants.ts for Service schema fallback
Schemas still validate after changes
Typecheck passes
Unit Tests: verify dateModified renders correctly in JSON-LD output
Code Review: run code-reviewer agent to verify schema validity
userStories.notes: Read tasks/docs/seo-aeo-geo-requirements.md (Requirement 8). Case study Article schemas have datePublished but not dateModified. Pillar Service schemas have no dates at all. Use general-purpose agent.

userStories.title: Update pillar page headings to question-form for AEO
userStories.description: As a content strategist, I need pillar pages to use question-form H2s so they appear in People Also Ask and AI Overviews.
acceptanceCriteria: Update 2-3 section H2s per sub-pillar page (12 pages total) to question form
Add a 1-2 sentence answer-first paragraph immediately after each question H2
First 150 words after each question H2 must be independently extractable as a standalone answer
Do NOT change headings where question form would be awkward
Existing content preserved; this is heading/lead-in rewrites only
No visual regression — styling unchanged
Typecheck passes
Code Review: run code-reviewer agent to verify content quality and heading hierarchy
userStories.notes: Read tasks/docs/seo-aeo-geo-requirements.md (Requirement 9) and tasks/docs/seo-aeo-geo-design.md (Question-Form H2 Content Strategy). Target the 12 sub-pillar pages in src/app/pillars/. Use general-purpose agent.

userStories.title: Homepage SSR refactor — server component with client children
userStories.description: As a site owner, I need the homepage to render meaningful HTML server-side so crawlers see real content instead of a blank page.
acceptanceCriteria: Remove 'use client' from src/app/page.tsx — make it a server component
Remove mounted state guard (if (!mounted) return null)
Split each content section into: server component (renders HTML content) + client component (adds animations)
Server-rendered HTML must include: <h1> with main headline, all section <h2> headings, CTA links, internal navigation links, key paragraph text
Animation-only effects (ASCIIBackground, ParticleField, MagneticCursor, AnimatedLogo) remain dynamic(() => ..., { ssr: false })
LenisProvider and ScrollAnimations wrapped in client boundary
All Framer Motion animations still work after hydration — no visual regression
GSAP ScrollTrigger animations still fire on scroll
Build succeeds with no hydration mismatch errors
Test: curl localhost:3000 | grep -i '<h1>' returns the homepage headline
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
Unit Tests: verify server component renders expected HTML structure without client JS
Code Review: run code-reviewer agent to verify SSR architecture and hydration safety
userStories.notes: Read tasks/docs/seo-aeo-geo-design.md (Homepage SSR Refactor Architecture — Islands pattern, Server Component Content Contract) and tasks/docs/seo-aeo-geo-plan.md (Phase 2, Task 7). This is the largest and riskiest item. Refactor one section at a time. Read src/app/page.tsx for current structure. Use frontend-expert agent.

userStories.title: Add FAQPage schema to homepage
userStories.description: As a site owner, I need FAQ structured data on the homepage to appear in Google's People Also Ask and AI Overviews.
acceptanceCriteria: Define 5-8 Q&A pairs about Claru services targeting high-value queries
Add <script type='application/ld+json'> with FAQPage schema in server-rendered homepage
Follow existing pattern from src/app/for-annotators/layout.tsx (lines 19-88)
Each answer is 40-80 words, self-contained, factually accurate
Schema validates with Google Rich Results Test
Typecheck passes
Unit Tests: verify FAQ JSON-LD structure with all Q&A pairs
Code Review: run code-reviewer agent to verify schema and content quality
userStories.notes: Read tasks/docs/seo-aeo-geo-requirements.md (Requirement 5). DEPENDS ON US-007 — homepage must be server component first. Use general-purpose agent.

userStories.title: Add FAQPage schema to data-catalog page
userStories.description: As a site owner, I need FAQ structured data on the data catalog page to improve search visibility.
acceptanceCriteria: Define 3-5 Q&A pairs about the data catalog
Add FAQPage JSON-LD to src/app/data-catalog/layout.tsx (or page)
Follow existing pattern from src/app/for-annotators/layout.tsx
Schema validates with Rich Results Test
Typecheck passes
Code Review: run code-reviewer agent to verify schema
userStories.notes: Read tasks/docs/seo-aeo-geo-requirements.md (Requirement 5). Independent of US-007. Use general-purpose agent.

userStories.title: Build dynamic OG image generator
userStories.description: As a site owner, I need dynamic Open Graph images for all pages so social shares show rich branded previews.
acceptanceCriteria: Create OG image route handler using Next.js ImageResponse API at src/app/api/og/route.tsx
Template design: dark background #0a0908, sage green #92B090 accent, JetBrains Mono font, Claru branding
Accept title and optional subtitle/category as query params
Output: 1200x630 PNG image
Bundle JetBrains Mono font as ArrayBuffer for edge runtime compatibility
Visit /api/og?title=Test renders a valid image
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
Unit Tests: verify route handler returns 200 with correct content-type
Code Review: run code-reviewer agent to verify edge runtime compatibility
userStories.notes: Read tasks/docs/seo-aeo-geo-design.md (Dynamic OG Image Generator) and tasks/docs/seo-aeo-geo-plan.md (Phase 4, Task 10). Test font loading early — JetBrains Mono ArrayBuffer bundling can be tricky. Use frontend-expert agent.

userStories.title: Wire OG images into all page metadata
userStories.description: As a site owner, I need every public page's metadata to reference the dynamic OG image generator.
acceptanceCriteria: Create shared helper src/lib/og.ts that generates the OG image URL for a given page title/category
Wire into root layout metadata.openGraph.images as default fallback
Wire into all 16 pillar/sub-pillar layout files with pillar-specific titles
Wire into case-studies/[slug]/page.tsx generateMetadata with case study title
Wire into jobs/[slug]/page.tsx generateMetadata with job title
Wire into remaining pages: data-catalog, for-annotators, labeling, data, case-studies listing, jobs listing
Update Twitter card type to summary_large_image on pages currently using summary
Build succeeds; <meta property='og:image'> renders correct URL on all pages
Typecheck passes
Code Review: run code-reviewer agent to verify all pages have OG images
userStories.notes: DEPENDS ON US-010. Read tasks/docs/seo-aeo-geo-design.md (buildPageMetadata helper). Use general-purpose agent.

userStories.title: Create entity consistency checklist
userStories.description: As a marketing lead, I need a documented checklist and canonical entity profile for cross-platform consistency.
acceptanceCriteria: Create canonical entity profile in src/lib/entity-profile.ts exporting: { name, legalName, description, tagline, url, email }
Use this object as single source of truth in Organization JSON-LD (US-002)
Create tasks/docs/entity-consistency-checklist.md listing platforms: LinkedIn, GitHub, Crunchbase, G2, Capterra
Include exact description text to copy-paste for each platform
Include sameAs URLs that should match Organization schema
Code Review: run code-reviewer agent to verify entity profile matches schema
userStories.notes: Read tasks/docs/seo-aeo-geo-requirements.md (Requirement 10). Partially non-code task. Use general-purpose agent.

userStories.title: Playwright E2E — SEO Validation Suite
userStories.description: As QA, I need automated tests verifying all SEO elements render correctly.
acceptanceCriteria: Create Playwright test file tests/e2e/seo-validation.spec.ts
Test: homepage has <h1> in server-rendered HTML
Test: homepage has Organization JSON-LD with name 'Claru'
Test: homepage has <link rel='canonical'>
Test: /robots.txt contains Sitemap: directive and does not block GPTBot
Test: /sitemap.xml returns XML with <urlset> containing 50+ <url> entries
Test: /llms.txt returns content with 'Claru' in body
Test: /case-studies has canonical link
Test: /pillars/acquire has FAQPage JSON-LD
Test: /api/og?title=Test returns image (content-type image/png)
Test: /for-annotators has og:image meta tag
Test uses Page Object Model pattern
Test runs against local dev server
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Use general-purpose agent with Playwright MCP.

userStories.title: Playwright MCP User Acceptance Testing
userStories.description: As QA, I need to perform user acceptance testing via Playwright MCP browser automation to verify all SEO changes render correctly and don't break the visual experience.
acceptanceCriteria: Start local dev server (npm run dev) if not already running
Use Playwright MCP to navigate to homepage — verify H1 renders, animations play after load, no blank page flash
Take screenshots at desktop (1440px) and mobile (375px) viewports
Navigate to /robots.txt — screenshot and verify content
Navigate to /sitemap.xml — screenshot and verify XML renders
Navigate to /llms.txt — verify content displays
Navigate to /pillars/acquire — verify question-form headings visible
Navigate to /case-studies — verify canonical tag in source
Navigate to /api/og?title=Claru%20Test — verify OG image renders
Test social share preview by inspecting og:image on 3 page types
Verify no visual regression on homepage animations (scroll full page)
Document any issues found and fix critical blockers
userStories.notes: This is the FINAL story. Dev server on localhost:3000. Use general-purpose agent with Playwright MCP.

userStories.title: Post-deploy GSC verification and indexing requests
userStories.description: As a site owner, after deploying all SEO changes I need to verify indexing via Google Search Console and request crawling of all pages.
acceptanceCriteria: After deploy: verify https://claru.ai/sitemap.xml returns valid XML
Use GSC MCP to verify sitemap status changed from 'pending' to 'success'
Use GSC MCP batch_url_inspection on all key pages to confirm crawlability
Use GSC MCP inspect_url_enhanced on homepage to verify no soft 404
Re-submit sitemap if needed
Check GSC performance overview after 3-7 days for initial indexing data
Document which pages are indexed vs pending
userStories.notes: Post-deploy task. Use GSC MCP tools: list_sitemaps_enhanced, batch_url_inspection, inspect_url_enhanced, get_performance_overview. Sitemap already submitted but shows errors until deploy. Use general-purpose agent.
